Bybit's involvement in this project signifies a significant step in revolutionizing cryptocurrency infrastructure and improving operational efficiency within the industry.

data.title: President Trump optimistic about nearing shutdown deal; markets uneasy. Crypto, gold, and silver trading cautiously amidst political uncertainty.
data.description: A potential agreement in Washington has alleviated concerns of a shutdown, yet critical votes are still unresolved. Consequently, crypto, gold, and silver markets are experiencing fluctuations due to liquidity concerns and risks associated with foreign policy.

data.title: Trump nominates Kevin Warsh as next Chair of Federal Reserve, succeeding Powell in May 2026. Warsh, former Fed governor, ranked among youngest governors in history. Born in 1970, Warsh has ties to Washington and Wall Street. served on Fed's Board of Governors from 2006 to 2011. During 2008 financial crisis, he acted as Fed's main liaison to Wall Street.
data.description: On Friday, President Donald Trump nominated Kevin Warsh to be the next Chair of the Federal Reserve, aiming to replace Jerome Powell when his term ends in May 2026. A former Federal Reserve governor, Warsh has extensive experience in finance and law and has strong connections in Washington and on Wall Street. From 2006 to 2011, he served as one of the youngest governors on the Fed's Board of Governors at just 35 years old. Born on April 13, 1970, in Albany, New York, Warsh holds a bachelor's degree in public policy from Stanford University, a Juris Doctor from Harvard Law School, and has completed additional coursework at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. During the 2008 financial crisis, Warsh played a key role as the Federal Reserve's main link to Wall Street and represented the central bank in various important capacities.
